title: Sweat on White Cotton
timeEstimate: 15–60 minutes; fresh + yellow-pit forks; widest bench but bounded chlorine for elastane
stainChemistry: Sweat on white cotton is two stains layered: fresh sweat (protein + skin oil + salt) which lifts easily with cool water + Dawn + enzyme, and the cumulative yellow pit stain (aluminum from antiperspirant + sweat protein) that builds over weeks/months and locks under heat. White cotton has the widest home bench (vinegar + Dawn + OxiClean + 3% peroxide + sun-dry + bounded chlorine), but the chlorine bleach option is bounded by elastane / colored trim presence (modern dress shirts almost always have elastane in collar / cuffs). Two hard rules across both forks: NO hot water (sets protein) and NO alcohol on sweat (sets protein beyond pro recovery).
whyThisWorks: Cool water keeps fresh sweat protein from setting. Dawn handles the skin-oil component. For yellow pit stains, vinegar's mild acid breaks the aluminum-sweat complex (the unlock that Dawn and OxiClean alone can't reach). OxiClean cool soak plus 3% peroxide handle residual yellow on white cotton. Sun-dry adds free UV photodegradation. Hot wash AFTER chemistry is safe on white cotton. The honest cap: years of hot-dried yellow pit buildup sometimes plateau even with full sequence; pro reducing-bleach is the next step.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): sweat is salt + sweat proteins + urea + sebum. Aluminum chlorohydrate (in antiperspirants) forms aluminum-protein complexes that yellow under heat. Pro uses dilute acetic acid (1:5–1:10) + NSD + 3% H₂O₂. Sodium hydrosulfite (reducing bleach) is the pro nuclear option for set yellow; chlorine bleach is bounded by elastane / trim presence.
defaultAssumption: FRESH (today's wear, damp shirt) = cool Dawn + enzyme handles it. YELLOW PIT (months / years of antiperspirant buildup) = full sequence with vinegar pretreat. Modern dress shirts and athletic wear with elastane in collar / cuffs = NO chlorine bleach.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Fresh sweat (today's wear) = cool Dawn + enzyme. Yellow pit stain (cumulative buildup) = vinegar pretreat FIRST, then full sequence.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold running water, white catch towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Fresh sweat only: turn inside out, cold flush from BACK 30–60 sec. Cool only — heat sets the protein.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Yellow pit stain only: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, press 10–15 min. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.
Generous Dawn. Soft toothbrush 5–10 min. Cotton tolerates warm water for the Dawn step. Damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Enzyme Pretreat or OxiClean Cool Soak
spottingProtocol.equipment: Enzyme pretreat or OxiClean per label, COOL water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Fresh: enzyme pretreat in COOL water 20 min. Yellow pit: OxiClean cool soak 30–60 min. NEVER mix with chlorine — toxic fumes.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 3% H₂O₂ on damp cloth or as soak additive
spottingProtocol.instruction: Yellow pit only: 3% peroxide damp cloth 10 min OR add to OxiClean soak. NEVER mix with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, hot water (per care label), sunlight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hot wash per care label — safe AFTER chemistry. Hang damp in direct sunlight 1–3 hours — UV photodegrades residual yellow. AIR-DRY (no hot dryer until cleared).

spottingProtocol.agent: Bounded Chlorine Bleach — Solid White Cotton, Last Resort
spottingProtocol.equipment: Chlorine bleach per label, hot wash
spottingProtocol.instruction: Bounded last resort for set yellow pit. ONLY if: (1) care label permits chlorine bleach, (2) garment is SOLID white cotton with no colored trim, (3) NO elastane / spandex / Lycra in collar / cuffs / waistband (chlorine destroys elastane permanently — most modern dress shirts have it), (4) NO other oxidizer in the same wash. Per label dilution. THOROUGHLY rinse vinegar / peroxide first — chlorine + acid = toxic chloramine gas.

steps: 3. White vinegar 1:1 cool pretreat for yellow pit, 10–15 min.
4. Dawn pretreat warm 5–10 min for oil component.
5. Enzyme cool 20 min (fresh) OR OxiClean cool soak 30–60 min (yellow pit).
6. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth or soak additive for stubborn yellow.
8. Bounded chlorine bleach only on solid white cotton, no elastane / trim, vinegar fully rinsed.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile / Biz / Tide Plus / liquid Protein Spotter)
Cool water for chemistry, hot wash AFTER
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool through chemistry; hot wash AFTER protein cleared
warnings: NEVER hot water during pre-wash chemistry — sets protein
NEVER alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery
NEVER chlorine bleach in same dwell as vinegar / acid — toxic chloramine gas
NEVER mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean — toxic fumes
Chlorine bleach: ONLY with care label permission AND solid white cotton AND no elastane AND no trim
Modern dress shirts almost always have elastane in collar / cuffs

homeSolutions.instruction: Sweat on white cotton has two cases that need different protocols. FRESH sweat (today's wear) = cool Dawn + enzyme. YELLOW PIT STAIN (months or years of antiperspirant buildup) = vinegar pretreat FIRST to break the aluminum-sweat bond, then the rest of the sequence. Two hard rules apply to both: NO hot water (sets the protein) and NO alcohol on sweat (sets the protein beyond pro recovery).
Fresh sweat: turn the shirt inside out, hold the BACK under cold running water 30 to 60 seconds. Cool only. Skip if it's set yellow pit buildup.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar Pretreat — Yellow Pit Only
homeSolutions.instruction: Yellow pit only: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, press 10 to 15 minutes. The mild acid breaks the aluminum-sweat complex. THIS is the unlock that makes the rest of the sequence work. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush 5 to 10 minutes. Cotton tolerates warm water — helps oil release. Damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme (Fresh) or OxiClean Soak (Yellow Pit) — Cool Water
homeSolutions.instruction: Fresh sweat: enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, Tide Plus) in COOL water 20 minutes. Yellow pit: OxiClean cool soak 30 to 60 minutes. Cool only — heat damages enzymes AND sets protein.
Yellow pit only: 3% peroxide on a damp cloth, press 10 minutes. OR add to the OxiClean soak.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.instruction: Hot machine wash per care label — safe AFTER chemistry. Hang damp in direct sunlight 1 to 3 hours — UV photodegrades residual yellow. AIR-DRY before any dryer.

homeSolutions.agent: Bounded Chlorine Bleach — Check Elastane First
homeSolutions.instruction: Set yellow pit that survived the full sequence: chlorine bleach is the home nuclear option but BOUNDED. ONLY use chlorine if (1) the care label permits, (2) the garment is SOLID white cotton with no colored trim, (3) NO elastane / spandex / Lycra in the collar / cuffs / waistband (chlorine destroys elastane — most modern dress shirts have it), and (4) NO other oxidizer in the same wash. THOROUGHLY rinse out any vinegar — chlorine + acid = toxic chloramine gas. Per label. Skip if any condition is uncertain.

homeSolutions.agent: Switch to Aluminum-Free Deodorant for Repeat Yellow Pit
homeSolutions.instruction: If you keep getting yellow pit stains, the cause is aluminum in your antiperspirant reacting with sweat. Aluminum-free deodorants prevent the yellow complex — switch for shirts you want to keep white. Skip fabric softener (locks aluminum in). Let deodorant dry completely before dressing.

steps: 2. NO hot water during chemistry. NO alcohol on sweat. NO chlorine + acid in same dwell.
4. White vinegar 1:1 cool 10–15 min for yellow pit (rinse before peroxide).
5. Dawn warm 5–10 min for skin oil.
6. Enzyme cool 20 min (fresh) OR OxiClean cool soak 30–60 min (yellow pit).
9. Bounded chlorine bleach only on solid white cotton, no elastane / trim.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, Tide Plus)
Cool water for chemistry, hot wash AFTER
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on yellow pit for years (often plateau). Stop if chlorine bleach already damaged collar elastane (irreversible). Stop if alcohol-based product was used on the sweat (often sets protein). Stop after full sequence + bounded chlorine plateau.
warnings: NEVER hot water during pre-wash chemistry — sets protein
NEVER alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery
NEVER chlorine bleach in same dwell as vinegar / acid — toxic chloramine gas
NEVER mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean
NEVER chlorine bleach on cotton with elastane
Modern dress shirts almost always have elastane in collar / cuffs

neverDo: Never hot water during pre-wash chemistry — sets protein
Never alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery
Never chlorine bleach in same dwell as vinegar / acid — toxic chloramine gas
Never mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean
Never chlorine bleach on cotton with elastane
Never iron over yellow pit — sets it
fiberSensitivities: Solid white cotton (no elastane, no trim): widest bench — vinegar, Dawn, OxiClean, 3% peroxide, sun-dry, bounded chlorine bleach, hot wash AFTER chemistry
White cotton with elastane (modern dress shirts, athletic wear): NO chlorine bleach
White cotton with colored trim: NO chlorine bleach
White cotton blend with polyester: cool only — heat sets oil into polyester

materialWarnings: Two-fork stain — fresh sweat vs yellow pit have different protocols
Vinegar is the unlock for yellow pit
No alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery

escalation.when: Years of hot-dried yellow pit buildup that survived full sequence. Chlorine bleach used on cotton with elastane (irreversible damage). Alcohol-based product was applied. Chlorine + vinegar/peroxide mixed (fume incident — ventilate, redo without mix).
escalation.whatToTell: "Sweat on [solid white / white-with-trim / white-with-elastane / blend] cotton, [fresh / yellow pit buildup, age weeks / months / years], home-treated with [cold back-flush + vinegar pretreat (yellow pit) + Dawn + enzyme/OxiClean + 3% peroxide + hot wash + sun-dry + bounded chlorine if eligible]. No hot pre-wash, no alcohol. [Residual yellow / collar elastane damaged / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional wet cleaner with reducing-bleach (sodium hydrosulfite) chemistry for set yellow pit

contextQuestions: Fresh sweat (today's wear) or yellow pit stain (months / years)?
Solid white cotton, white with colored trim, white with elastane, or blend?
Has chlorine bleach been used on the spot? On cotton with elastane?
Has alcohol-based product (hand sanitizer, body spray) been applied?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: The fresh-vs-yellow-pit fork is the entire job. Fresh sweat is easy; yellow pit is the cumulative aluminum-sweat lock that needs the vinegar pretreat. Modern dress shirts almost always contain elastane — chlorine destroys it permanently. Aluminum-free deodorant is the prevention path.
